strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Oregon is a Tier 1 public research institution known for its collaborative campus culture, robust Division I athletic spirit, and prominent programs in business, journalism, and environmental sciences. As a test-optional institution, UO utilizes a holistic admissions process, offering accessible entry to the university at large while maintaining competitive, direct-admit pathways for specific programs like Architecture and Business.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Overall admission is highly accessible with an ~88% acceptance rate, but specialized programs like Architecture and Business operate as highly selective 'direct-admit' or 'pre-major' silos.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants should not fear rejection from the university if they apply to a competitive major, as they will still be admitted as 'exploring' (undeclared) if they meet general university standards.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Non-resident tuition and fees are steep ($44,598), resulting in an out-of-state COA exceeding $63,000, though strong automatic merit scholarships like the UO Excellence ($20k/yr) help offset costs.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Out-of-state applicants with strong academic records (3.90+ GPA) can expect significant automatic tuition discounts.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The Clark Honors College offers a liberal arts college experience within a massive research university, complete with small seminar classes and a thesis requirement.
analyst_takeaways.implication: High-achieving students seeking close faculty mentorship should apply to the Honors College to bypass the large lecture sizes typical of lower-division classes.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant and outdoors-oriented; heavy emphasis on Big Ten Division I sports (Oregon Ducks), over 250 student organizations, a bustling Greek life presence, and an active campus recreation culture.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Highly bike-friendly campus located within an urban setting in Eugene, with reliable local bus transit (Lane Transit District) accessible to students.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as R1 (Very High Research Activity). Over 73% of undergraduates engage in research, supported by prominent facilities like the Phil and Penny Knight Campus for Accelerating Scientific Impact.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-optional since Fall 2021. SAT/ACT scores are not required for admission or most merit scholarships. They are considered equally if submitted but play little to no role in the primary holistic review.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Yield rate has been steadily declining over a 10-year period, reaching 14.4% in 2024.

gpa.notes: GPA reflects high school cumulative averages. 54% of enrolled students had a 3.75+ GPA.

testing.policy_note: Test-optional since 2021; very few applicants submit scores.

colleges.name: Lundquist College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students interested in sports product management, finance, and entrepreneurship. Apply as 'pre-business' and maintain a 3.0 GPA to guarantee major declaration.
colleges.housing_profile: Academic Residential Communities (ARCs) are available to integrate living and major-specific learning.

majors.name: Business Administration
majors.special_requirements: Requires 75 credits and a 3.0 cumulative GPA, plus a 3.0 in lower-division core for guaranteed admission.

core_courses: BA 169Z Data Analysis Using Microsoft Excel

majors.notes: Direct admission is offered to select high-achieving high school seniors. All others must enter as Pre-Business and pass holistic or guaranteed review.

colleges.name: College of Design (School of Architecture and Environment)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking rigorous, studio-based architectural training focused on sustainability and healthy built environments.
colleges.student_archetype: Creative, technically proficient, and environmentally conscious.

majors.name: Architecture
majors.special_requirements: Requires a creative portfolio and two essay statements. Departmental application review is separate from the university review.

core_courses: ARCH 222 Introduction to Architectural Computer Graphics

majors.notes: If a student is denied the Architecture major, they are still admitted to UO as 'exploring' (undeclared).

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Great for students pursuing STEM, humanities, or social sciences. Allows exploration before committing to a major.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard residence halls with various Academic Residential Communities.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.special_requirements: Must receive a mid-C or better in PSY 201Z and PSY 202Z to count toward major.
majors.notes: Requires 16 upper-division credits to be taken in residence at UO.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.notes: Intro sequences transition from Python (CS 210/211) to C (CS 212).

application_deadlines.notes: Non-binding Early Action. Decisions released by December 15.
Regular decision deadline. Decisions released by April 1.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires a creative portfolio and two departmental essay statements.
Requires audition and musicianship placement examinations.
A special circumstances statement is optional but encouraged to explain any academic hindrances.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: The UO uses a test-optional approach where SAT/ACT scores genuinely play little to no role if omitted.

major_selection_tactics: Apply directly to selective majors (Business, Architecture) without fear of general rejection. Denied students are simply placed in an 'exploring' (undeclared) or 'pre-major' track.
If denied direct admission to Business, apply as Pre-Business and follow the guaranteed admission track (maintain a 3.0 cumulative and core GPA).
Submit applications Early Action to secure priority consideration for merit scholarships like the Summit and Apex awards.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: The University of Oregon allows applicants to be considered for their second/alternate major if they are not admitted to a highly selective first-choice major. Furthermore, missing a direct admit spots places you in the Pre-Major track for standard internal progression.

scholarships.name: Stamps Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers full tuition, fees, room, and board for in-state; out-of-state receives tuition and fees. Includes $12,000 in enrichment funds and automatic admission to the Honors College.

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awards $36,000 over four years to top Oregon residents with a 3.85+ GPA.

scholarships.name: UO Excellence Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awards $80,000 over four years to top non-resident applicants.

scholarships.name: Summit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Automatic award for students with a 3.90+ GPA. $12,500/year for non-residents; $5,000/year for residents.

scholarships.name: Apex Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Automatic award based on academic performance for residents and non-residents.

scholarships.name: PathwayOregon
scholarships.benefits: Ensures full tuition and fees are covered for Pell Grant-eligible Oregon residents.

ap_policy.general_rule: AP credits are awarded P* marks (no letter grade) for scores of 3, 4, or 5 depending on the exam.
ap_policy.usage: Counts toward the 168 hours degree requirement and may satisfy major prerequisites or Honors College requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Scores of 5+ on recognized Higher Level (HL) exams grant credit.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: A maximum of 124 transfer credits from a community college can be applied to a bachelor's degree. Students must complete 16 upper-division credits in residence for certain majors.

what_it_takes: A strong high school GPA (3.7+) with rigorous college prep courses.
Applying Early Action to maximize chances for automatic merit scholarships.
Direct applications to competitive majors like Architecture or Business.
common_activities: Outdoor activities (hiking, skiing, going to the coast)
Attending Oregon Ducks D1 athletic events
essay_tips: Explain any academic drops or hindrances in the optional special circumstances essay.
Highlight leadership, perseverance, and service to compete for top scholarships like the Stamps.
red_flags: Missing the November 1 Early Action deadline if out-of-state, severely limiting merit aid.
Attempting to bypass pre-major requirements with poor performance in filter/weeder classes.
insights: Students appreciate the high quality of campus dining and dorms.
The 'Reverse Transfer' program allows students to earn their associate's degree using credits earned at UO after transferring from a community college.
The college town environment of Eugene is heavily praised for accessibility, public transit, and natural beauty.
